summary: Frog Music Press, led by organist Noel Jones (AAGO) out of Augusta, KY, publishes Catholic liturgical music resources: organ and choir books, Gregorian chant guides, MIDI organ files, the PTCO series for Rodgers and Roland digital organs, and a 14-week Free Chant School. Materials are distributed mostly as free PDF downloads (with a donation ask) plus low-cost Amazon paperbacks and Kindle editions. It serves amateur and part-time Catholic parish organists and choir directors who need affordable, liturgically accurate music without formal training.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new free-download subscriber to their first 'aha' — successfully singing or playing one chant or hymn — within the first week, then frame future donations and books around the mission.
campaigns.trigger: New subscriber: free PDF downloaded, Free Chant School signup, or list join. Send email 1 within seconds.
campaigns.segment: All new contacts; branch by entry point (chant learner vs. organ/PTCO player vs. choir director).
campaigns.rationale: The brand's biggest leak is that free users grab one PDF and vanish with no follow-up; an instant welcome that delivers a quick win is the single highest-leverage flow to convert anonymous downloaders into engaged, eventually-donating parish musicians.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, set expectations, and deliver one immediate win — the first chant lesson or first playable organ page — so the new subscriber experiences value the same day they sign up.
emails.subject: Welcome to Frog Music Press — your first chant is inside
emails.preview: Two minutes to sing your first line of Gregorian chant. No degree required.
emails.cta: Start Lesson 1 of the Free Chant School
keyPoints: Warm, devotional welcome from Noel Jones, AAGO — a fellow musician in the choir loft, not a marketer
Point them to one specific first action: open Lesson 1 (or, for organists, the opening pages of a PTCO book) and play/sing through it today
Set the expectation that a few short, useful emails are coming — and invite a real reply if they get stuck
Frame the work as ministry: 'everything here exists so you can lead music at Mass, whatever your parish budget'

campaigns.name: Free Chant School Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move a Free Chant School enrollee from problem-recognition ('I can't read chant notation') through the 14-week curriculum toward leading chant at Mass and, ultimately, buying a print chant title or donating.
campaigns.trigger: Free Chant School enrollment or download of a chant lead magnet (Free Music Shelf, chant sample pages).
campaigns.segment: Chant learners below the engaged/buyer threshold; branch by progress through the curriculum and by role (organist vs. choir director vs. teacher).
campaigns.rationale: The Free Chant School is the brand's strongest lead magnet but almost certainly has no email follow-through, so learners stall mid-curriculum; a stage-matched nurture mirrors the 14 weeks, keeps them progressing to the aha moment, and creates the natural moment to offer a print edition or next book.

emails.purpose: Deliver the curriculum and frame the real problem it solves — turning unfamiliar four-line chant notation into music you can confidently lead — and commit the learner to a weekly rhythm.
emails.subject: Your Free Chant School starts now — Lesson 1 of 14
emails.preview: By Sunday you'll read your first line of square-note notation.
keyPoints: Confirm enrollment and deliver the Lesson 1 link plus the full 14-week roadmap so they know exactly where this leads
Name the pain directly: square-note chant notation looks intimidating, but it's actually simpler than modern staff notation — and you'll prove it this week
Set a gentle weekly cadence ('one lesson a week gets your choir singing chant before the next liturgical season')
Reassure on authority: taught on video by Noel Jones, AAGO, written for true beginners with no pressure

campaigns.name: Win-Back & Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant subscribers who downloaded once and went silent, and clean the list to protect deliverability for the small sending operation.
campaigns.trigger: No opens or clicks for 90 days (or downloaded a single PDF and never returned).
campaigns.segment: Dormant subscribers and one-time downloaders; lean on new-publication releases as the re-engagement hook since there is no renewal signal.
campaigns.rationale: Silent disengagement is explicitly the brand's core churn problem — there's no subscription to trigger a renewal — so a win-back is the only mechanism to recover the large pool of one-time free downloaders, and re-engagement typically recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with a warm, no-guilt 'we miss you in the choir loft', remind them of the core value, and use a fresh publication or MIDI release as the reason to come back.
emails.subject: Still leading music at your parish?
emails.preview: It's been a while — here's what's new in the choir loft since you left.
keyPoints: Open warmly and pastorally — acknowledge that parish musicians are busy and volunteer time is scarce, no guilt
Remind them of what they came for: free, liturgically correct chant and organ music for Catholic worship
Lead with a concrete new hook — a newly released volume, chant title, or MIDI library addition added since they last visited
Make returning a single low-friction click, and signal email 4 will quietly ask whether to stop emailing (sunset) if there's no response

campaigns.name: The Choir Loft Newsletter
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long, non-recurring cycle and reinforce Noel Jones's authority by delivering one genuinely useful teaching idea per issue, tied to the liturgical calendar.
campaigns.trigger: Scheduled monthly cadence, timed to the liturgical season (Advent, Lent, Easter, Ordinary Time).
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding (Campaign 1) and route 90-day dormant contacts to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: The brand already produces newsletter-worthy content (new Quarterly volumes, MIDI updates, teaching insight) but likely doesn't distribute it consistently, so an always-on newsletter is the retention engine that keeps free downloaders engaged between releases and primes them to donate or buy.

emails.purpose: Establish the flagship issue format — one practical teaching tip, a seasonal repertoire suggestion, and a new resource — using the upcoming fall liturgical season as the timely hook.
emails.subject: 3 chant settings to prepare before the fall season
emails.preview: Plus a new Salve Regina MIDI file and one organ registration tip.
keyPoints: One useful idea per send: a single practical teaching tip (e.g., registering soft foundation stops for accompanying chant)
Seasonal repertoire block: 3 specific chant or hymn settings to rehearse now so the choir is ready for the fall liturgical calendar
Recurring 'New on the shelf' section spotlighting a fresh free download or MIDI library addition, with an 80/20 value-to-soft-ask balance
Single primary CTA (read/listen), warm scholarly voice, and a soft mission-framed donation line in the footer rather than the lead

campaigns.name: New Volume & Series Expansion
campaigns.objective: Drive repeat engagement and revenue from past buyers and donors by promoting newly released seasonal volumes and encouraging completion of The Catholic Organist's Quarterly series.
campaigns.trigger: New publication release (seasonal Quarterly volume or new chant/organ title); past purchase of an earlier volume in a series.
campaigns.segment: Past Amazon buyers and donors; branch by what they already own (series-completion gap) and by instrument (organ/PTCO vs. choral/chant).
campaigns.rationale: With 9+ Quarterly volumes (Fall, Winter, Spring, Summer, Manuals-Only) there's a built-in series-completion upsell, but no trigger currently promotes new releases to past buyers — making this the brand's clearest expansion play in the absence of any subscription renewal.

emails.purpose: Announce a new seasonal volume to past buyers, recap the value of the volumes they already have, and frame completing the seasonal set as the natural next step.
emails.subject: The new Catholic Organist's Quarterly (Fall) is here
emails.preview: You have Summer — the Fall volume completes your seasonal set.
emails.cta: Get the Fall volume on Amazon
keyPoints: Lead with the new release and what's inside (organ settings, choral pieces, and chant for the fall liturgical season)
Acknowledge the volume(s) they already own and position Fall as completing the four-season set on the music rack
Reinforce authority and consistency: same trusted editing and liturgical accuracy from Noel Jones across the series
Offer both paths respectfully — buy the print/Kindle edition, or grab the free PDF and donate if it serves their parish
